thesis: Pricing pages confuse buyers because teams optimize feature lists before they set the comparison anchor
audience_belief_to_challenge: Buyers evaluate pricing tiers by counting features
core_mechanism: Anchoring changes perceived value before feature details are compared

proof_anchors:
---
proof_id: proof_01
summary: Studies show the first price seen creates a reference point that distorts all subsequent evaluation
source_ids: src_01, src_02
usage_note: Use in the hook to establish the core mechanism
---
---
proof_id: proof_02
summary: The decoy effect is strongest when one tier makes the target tier feel more reasonable
source_ids: src_01, src_03
usage_note: Use in the reframe section
---

counterarguments:
---
counterargument_id: ca_01
counterargument: Anchoring only works for luxury or enterprise pricing
response: The effect has been replicated across consumer SaaS and SMB pricing pages
response_proof_ids: src_04
---

safe_claims:
---
claim_id: claim_01
claim: Anchoring changes perceived value when the higher-price option is presented first
supporting_proof_ids: proof_01, proof_02
---
---
claim_id: claim_02
claim: The comparison set buyers use is often different from what designers assume
supporting_proof_ids: src_02
---

unsafe_claims:
---
claim_id: unsafe_01
claim: Changing the anchor will guarantee a conversion lift
supporting_proof_ids:
note: Too strong — the evidence only shows directional correlation, not causation
---

beat_claim_plan:
---
beat_id: beat_01
beat_name: Hook
goal: Make the pricing mistake feel obvious in retrospect
claim_ids: claim_01
proof_anchor_ids: proof_01
counterargument_ids:
transition_note: Lead with the familiar mistake to activate the belief
---
---
beat_id: beat_02
beat_name: Reframe
goal: Show the comparison anchor buyers actually use
claim_ids: claim_02
proof_anchor_ids: proof_02
counterargument_ids: ca_01
transition_note: Shift from feature count to anchor comparison
---
---
beat_id: beat_03
beat_name: CTA
goal: Prompt one concrete change to the pricing card
claim_ids: claim_01
proof_anchor_ids:
counterargument_ids:
transition_note: Keep it simple — one anchor, one change
---

genericity_flags:
- pricing_psychology_general
- smb_saas_specific

what_this_contributes: A concrete, page-level pricing anchor example that makes the abstract mechanism visible

differentiation_strategy: Focus on the BEFORE state — most content shows the fix, we show the mistake buyers make first
